turns.content: Good afternoon, and welcome to the Walmart Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This quarter demonstrated resilience across our business despite a mixed macroeconomic environment. In Q1 2024, operating income was largely stable compared to the prior quarter, reflecting balanced supply and demand dynamics. Our advertising business strategy continues to gain traction, with Walmart Connect showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in advertising business because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from marketplace validate our approach. While we remain cautious about near-term macro uncertainty, our fundamentals are solid.

Thank you, Doug. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2024 was $74.2 billion, representing growth of 8% year over year. Our comp sales reached $11.8 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 30.5%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $19.8 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $5 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Walmart U.S., we saw 13% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Mark. Great question. On inventory levels, we are pleased with the progress on supply chain automation and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Walmart International to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On Walmart International, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Wamsi. Great question. On operating income, we are pleased with the progress on supply chain automation and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Walmart International to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Walmart Connect?

Sure, Kash. The inventory levels trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Brad. Great question. On inventory levels, we are pleased with the progress on advertising business and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Walmart International to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Walmart Inc. Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This was a record-breaking quarter for the company, reflecting the strength of our strategy and execution. In Q2 2024, We saw 6% growth in Walmart International, well above our internal targets. Our supply chain automation strategy continues to gain traction, with Walmart International showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in supply chain automation because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from e-commerce validate our approach. Looking ahead, we see continued strength in demand and expect to deliver above-trend growth next quarter.

Thank you, Doug. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2024 was $20.1 billion, representing growth of 11% year over year. Our comp sales reached $13.6 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 23.6%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $11.4 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $11 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Walmart U.S., we saw 19% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating income trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Brent. Great question. On operating income, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in supply chain automation has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Walmart U.S. to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating income trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Kash. The operating income trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On Walmart International, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Keith. The inventory levels trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On inventory levels, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in omnichannel growth has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Walmart U.S. to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Walmart Inc. Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

While we made progress on key strategic initiatives, our financial results were below our expectations. In Q3 2024, We saw a 27% decline in Walmart Connect as customers delayed purchasing decisions amid macro uncertainty. Our omnichannel growth strategy continues to gain traction, with Walmart Connect showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in omnichannel growth because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from grocery delivery validate our approach. We are taking a more measured approach to our outlook and adjusting our spending accordingly.

Thank you, Doug. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $18.9 billion, representing growth of 4% year over year. Our comp sales reached $44.7 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 32.7%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $13.8 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $14 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Walmart U.S., we saw 19% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating income trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Kash. The operating income trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Walmart U.S.?

Thanks, Toni. Great question. On operating income, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Walmart U.S. for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Walmart U.S. to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On Walmart U.S., can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Keith. The inventory levels trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Walmart U.S.?

Sure, Amit. The inventory levels trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Walmart Inc. Q4 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered solid results this quarter, broadly in line with our expectations. In Q4 2024, inventory levels was largely stable compared to the prior quarter, reflecting balanced supply and demand dynamics. Our price leadership strategy continues to gain traction, with Walmart International showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in price leadership because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from grocery delivery validate our approach. For the upcoming quarter, we expect results roughly in line with seasonal patterns.

Thank you, Doug. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q4 2024 was $114.5 billion, representing growth of 30% year over year. Our comp sales reached $30.5 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 38.9%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $16.8 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $9 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Walmart U.S., we saw 9% growth, driven by strong demand fundamentals.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the inventory levels trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Shannon. The inventory levels trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the comp sales trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Raimo. The comp sales trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Brad. The inventory levels trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Great, thanks. On Walmart Connect, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Raimo. Great question. On comp sales, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in supply chain automation has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Walmart Connect to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to John for the specific numbers.
